title: Grant Integrity and Border Security Act
short_title: Grant Integrity and Border Security Act
plain_summary: This bill makes new rules for people who want to get money from the government through grants. Before getting a grant, people must promise they have not helped bring people into the United States illegally or hidden people who are in the country illegally for the past 10 years. They also must promise not to do these things while they have the grant. If the government finds out someone broke these rules, they will take away the grant money. The Department of Justice must tell budget officials about people who break these immigration laws.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Grant Integrity and Border Security Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Federal Grant Requirement Related to the Bringing In and Harboring of Certain Aliens
sections.text:
Part a says that government agencies must require anyone applying for federal grant money to make a promise in writing. This promise must say three things. First, the applicant has not broken immigration law section two hundred seventy four a in the past ten years. Second, they are not currently breaking this law. Third, they will not break this law while they have the grant money. Immigration law section two hundred seventy four a makes it illegal to help people enter the United States illegally or to hide people who are in the country illegally.

Part b says that government agencies can take away grant money if they find out a grant recipient is breaking these rules. Agencies can make this decision based on three types of information. First, they can use information given to them by the Secretary of Homeland Security or the Director of the Office of Management and Budget. Second, they can act if any current or former employee of the grant recipient is convicted of breaking immigration law section two hundred seventy four a while doing their official job duties, or if that employee admits to breaking this law. Third, they can use any other believable information they receive.

Part c requires the Attorney General to report certain information to the Director of the Office of Management and Budget. This must happen within ninety days after someone is convicted of breaking immigration law section two hundred seventy four a, admits to breaking this law, or after an investigation about this law is finished. The Attorney General must share information about anyone who was convicted or admitted to breaking this law. The Attorney General must also share information about anyone they reasonably believe broke this law.

Part d explains what certain words mean in this law. Agency means the same thing it means in title five section five hundred fifty one of federal law. Director means the Director of the Office of Management and Budget. Federal grant means the same thing as federal award in title thirty one section six thousand four hundred one of federal law.
